paper_title: Model and Theoretical Method for Diatomic Molecule with Electron-Phonon Interaction

methods.description: This method describes the calculation of local retarded Green's functions to analyze electron transport through a diatomic molecule model with electron-phonon interactions.

steps.description: Define the Hamiltonian for the diatomic molecule with local electron-electron interactions and electron-phonon inter-site interaction.
steps.searchable_summary: define Hamiltonian for diatomic molecule with interactions

steps.description: Transform the Hamiltonian using symmetric and antisymmetric fermionic operators.
Apply the equation of motion technique to obtain Green's functions.
steps.searchable_summary: apply equation of motion technique for Green's functions

steps.description: Use Hubbard-like decoupling procedure for mixed Green's functions.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Hubbard-like decoupling for mixed Green's functions

steps.description: Calculate electron and phonon self-energies up to second order in interaction strength.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate self-energies for electron and phonon interactions

steps.description: Calculate the density of states (DOS) using the Green's functions.
Determine resonance conditions for electron-phonon interactions.
steps.searchable_summary: determine resonance conditions for electron-phonon interactions

steps.description: Calculate transport properties using the Keldysh formalism.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate transport properties using Keldysh formalism
